rationale_for_baseline_tier: A dehydrated patient unable to drink for 18h needs urgent same-day in-person assessment for rehydration and analgesia plus antivirals; escalate to T4 (ED/IV fluids) if oral rehydration fails or AKI features develop.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Unable to swallow any fluids, no urine output for >8h, or dizziness/confusion on standing
escalation_edges.rationale: Signals significant dehydration/AKI requiring IV fluids.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you can't swallow any fluids, stop passing urine for more than about 8 hours, or feel dizzy or confused — go to the emergency department for a drip.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Skin peeling, target lesions, or new eye/genital sores
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your skin starts peeling, you get bullseye spots, or sores in your eyes or genitals — go to the emergency department straight away.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Spouse to help with the fluid trial and arrange urgent transport if the patient can't rehydrate.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Start the antiviral tablets and pain relief, use the numbing mouthwash before sipping cold fluids continuously, and get reviewed in person today for hydration. If you can't drink or stop passing urine, go to ED for a drip.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Same-day in-person review; ED for IV fluids if unable to rehydrate
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: After-hours GP for review; ED for inability to drink, no urine output, or dizziness.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document primary herpetic gingivostomatitis with dehydration, antivirals/analgesia commenced, SJS screened negative, and hydration plan.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Antivirals and home advice only, without addressing dehydration/inability to drink.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Dehydration progresses to AKI and collapse before help is sought.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Routine handling that delays hydration and analgesia.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Calling an ambulance when same-day in-person review/ED self-transfer would suffice and there is no collapse/airway issue.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot examine the mouth/airway or objectively assess hydration remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat as primary HSV gingivostomatitis, provide antivirals/analgesia, and prioritise urgent rehydration/assessment.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Antivirals + home advice while underplaying the dehydration risk.
